data.acceptance:
1. Child knots exist for protocol design, shared runtime/interface work, and each of Codex, OpenCode, Gemini, and Copilot interactive-session implementations.
2. The decomposition preserves a concrete protocol for turn completion, session completion, watchdog timeout, retry, and abort semantics.
3. The resulting plan removes hard-coded Claude-only interactivity assumptions in favor of declared agent capabilities.
4. Child knots define enough scope to implement and verify the protocol without inventing a second tracker.
